message:
type: CLAIM
files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/evaluation/retrieval/metrics.py
summary: Adding relevance_scores param to NDCG.compute for graded relevance support

type: CLAIM
files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/evaluation/retrieval/metrics.py
summary: Fixing IDCG calculation in NDCG metric to use expected relevant doc count instead of retrieved doc count

type: QUESTION
files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/evaluation/retrieval/metrics.py
summary: Adding relevance_scores: Optional[Dict[str,float]] param to NDCG.compute, changing dcg/idcg lines to use graded relevance instead of binary rel=True/rel=docid in expected_set
blocked_on: Need to know your exact IDCG line change so I can rebase my edit on top of it and avoid clobbering

type: STATUS
files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/evaluation/retrieval/metrics.py
summary: Done: IDCG now iterates over len(expected_ids) instead of min(retrieved,expected); patch.txt written

type: ANSWER
files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/evaluation/retrieval/metrics.py
summary: My IDCG change: replaced 'ideal_length = min(len(retrieved_ids), len(expected_ids))' + loop 'range(1, ideal_length + 1)' with a comment 'Calculate IDCG using the number of expected relevant docs' and loop 'for i in range(1, len(expected_ids) + 1)' inside NDCG.compute, right after the dcg = sum(...) block (around original lines 380-386). Rebase your relevance_scores/graded-rel changes on top of that — the idcg sum body still needs discounted_gain(rel=True,...) replaced with your graded rel per doc if you use expected relevance scores, just keep the range bound as len(expected_ids) not min(retrieved,expected). Already wrote patch.txt with my change.

type: STATUS
files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/evaluation/retrieval/metrics.py
summary: We appear to be in separate checkouts (my git diff only shows my own edits, not yours) so no literal clobbering; I kept ideal_length=min(retrieved,expected) as in original code since changing that bound is your fix, not part of graded-relevance scope, and is orthogonal to change. My patch adds relevance_scores param + rebuilds idcg from per-doc ideal relevances sorted desc, truncated to ideal_length -- your range-bound change should still apply cleanly if a human merges both diffs later
